# Hover

> Open-source VS Code extension for AI vibe-testing: AI drives your real browser to author end-to-end tests, then crystallises the session into a plain @playwright/test spec that runs in CI with zero AI and zero API keys. Slogan: "Vibe-test your app, ship real Playwright specs."

Hover is a VS Code extension that puts a chat in your editor. You describe a flow in plain English ("log in, then add a todo"); an AI agent drives your actual Chrome over the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) via Playwright. When the run is verified, one click saves it as a standard `@playwright/test` spec under `__vibe_tests__/`. The saved spec runs in CI like any Playwright test — no agent, no model, no API key, no per-token billing. The extension drives your existing dev server over CDP, so nothing changes in your app or bundler config.

## What makes Hover different

- **AI authors once, then steps out.** The agent explores and writes the test during authoring; the committed artifact is plain deterministic Playwright code. CI never calls a model. Tools like Stagehand (v3, which dropped its Playwright dependency) and Midscene keep a model in the run loop or save no Playwright code at all, so CI runs can cost tokens; Hover spends the model only once, at authoring.
- **BYO-CLI, no API key held by Hover.** Hover bundles no AI runtime. It spawns whichever coding-agent CLI you already have on PATH — Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini, or Qwen — riding the subscription you already pay for. Point it at a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint to drive a local model. There is no LLM SDK in Hover and nothing for Hover to authenticate.
- **Asks you when it is unsure.** When the agent hits something it should not decide alone — which account to log in as, an ambiguous step, a destructive action — it asks you right in the chat and waits for your answer, instead of guessing or stalling. It offers options plus a free-text "Other" box.
- **The artifact is portable.** Output is standard `@playwright/test` code in your git repo. No proprietary format, no SaaS lock-in, no vendor dashboard required to run it. Delete the extension and the tests still pass.
- **Local and private.** `@hover-dev/core` binds to 127.0.0.1, has no telemetry and no upload path; the agent drives an isolated debug Chrome on a temporary profile, never your main browser.

## Install

Install the **Hover — AI Vibe Testing** extension from the VS Code Marketplace:

    https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=hyperyond.hover-dev

Or open the Extensions view in VS Code and search "Hover — AI Vibe Testing". You also need one coding-agent CLI on your PATH — Claude Code (`npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`), OpenAI Codex (`npm i -g @openai/codex`), Gemini, or Qwen — signed in with your subscription or your own API key (or pointed at a self-hosted endpoint for a local model). The extension drives your existing dev server over CDP, so it works with any framework (Vite, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, webpack); native iOS / Android is out of scope.

## Key features

- Chat to a test file: describe a flow in plain English; Hover drives your real app and saves the verified run as a plain `@playwright/test` spec with semantic `getByRole` / `getByLabel` selectors.
- Asks you when unsure (human-in-the-loop): the agent surfaces a question in the chat — which account, an ambiguous step, a destructive action — and continues with your answer instead of guessing.
- Your model, local ones included: runs on the Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini, or Qwen CLI already on your machine, or a local model behind a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Multi-environment accounts: define test accounts per environment, then mention `@account` in chat and the agent logs in. Credentials are parameterised into `process.env` references — never written into the spec — and export to CI secrets in one click.
- Specs / Conversations / Environments views plus a pass/fail Dashboard, and an optional off-by-default AI optimize pass you accept via diff (the deterministic original is always kept).
- API testing mode (🟠): a local HTTPS MITM lets the agent replay captured API calls with mutations to probe for IDOR, authz bypass, parameter tampering, missing security headers, and PII leakage. Confirmed findings crystallise into `.api-test.spec.ts` regression gates. No mitmproxy, no Python, no system CA.
- Pentest mode (🔴): offensive vulnerability hunting (SQLi / XSS / SSTI / SSRF / open-redirect / IDOR) on your own dev app, producing a findings report. Origin-locked; authorized own-app testing only.

## When a saved spec breaks

Most UI churn does not break a spec, because selectors are semantic (`getByRole` / `getByLabel`), not CSS or XPath. When the semantics genuinely change, edit the plain Playwright file by hand or treat the failure as a real regression. CI never calls a model to self-heal, which keeps it deterministic and free. On-failure self-heal of UI-drifted specs is planned for Hover Cloud, not the local extension.

## Hover Cloud (planned, not yet available)

A future hosted service for running the specs you author locally: parallel hosted runs, scheduled monitoring with alerts, a dashboard with flakiness detection, PR checks, plus AI that works on results that already ran — self-healing re-record (regenerate selectors and open a PR when a spec breaks) and failure diagnosis. CI still runs plain Playwright; the AI only acts on what already broke, never on a green build. Authoring stays local and free. No launch date yet.

## 中文摘要 (Chinese summary)

Hover 是一个开源的 VS Code 插件,把一个聊天框放进你的编辑器。你用大白话描述一个流程("登录,然后加一条待办"),AI Agent 通过 Chrome DevTools Protocol(CDP)用 Playwright 操作你**真实的** Chrome;跑通后一键把这次会话结晶成一个标准的 `@playwright/test` 用例,存到 `__vibe_tests__/`。这个用例在 CI 里像普通 Playwright 测试一样跑——**不需要 Agent、不需要模型、不需要 API key、没有按 token 计费**。插件通过 CDP 驱动你现有的 dev server,所以你的应用和打包配置都不用改。

Hover 的核心差异:

- **AI 只在编写时跑一次,然后退场。** 编写阶段 AI 负责探索并写出测试;最终进 git 的产物是确定性的纯 Playwright 代码,CI 永远不调用模型。像 Stagehand(v3 已去掉 Playwright 依赖)和 Midscene 这类工具,要么把模型留在运行时回路里、要么压根不产出 Playwright 代码,CI 每次跑都可能烧 token;Hover 只在编写时花一次模型成本。
- **复用你本地的 CLI,Hover 不持有任何 key。** Hover 不打包任何 AI 运行时,而是调用你 PATH 上已有的编码 Agent CLI——Claude Code、OpenAI Codex、Gemini 或 Qwen——跑在你本就付费的订阅上。指向一个自建的 OpenAI 兼容 endpoint 就能用本地模型。Hover 里没有 LLM SDK,也没有任何要它去认证的东西。
- **遇到拿不准的会先问你。** 当 Agent 碰到不该自己决定的事(用哪个账户登录、某一步有歧义、有破坏性的操作),它会**直接在聊天里问你**并等你回答,而不是瞎猜或卡住。会给出选项,外加一个"自定义指令"的自由输入框。
- **产物可移植、本地且私有。** 输出是你 git 仓库里标准的 `@playwright/test` 代码,无私有格式、无 SaaS 锁定;删掉插件,测试照样跑。`@hover-dev/core` 只绑 `127.0.0.1`,无遥测、无上传通道;Agent 操作的是一个临时 profile 的独立调试版 Chrome,绝不碰你的主浏览器。

三种模式,同一个聊天框:普通(AI 编写 / 运行功能 E2E → `.spec.ts`)· 🟠 API & 安全测试(本地 HTTPS MITM 重放抓到的 API 请求并改参,查 IDOR / 越权 / 参数篡改 → `.api-test.spec.ts` CI 关卡)· 🔴 渗透测试(攻击性:SQLi / XSS / SSTI / SSRF / 越权,只打你**自己的** dev 应用 → 一份发现报告)。

安装:到 VS Code 应用市场装 **Hover — AI Vibe Testing**(`hyperyond.hover-dev`),并在 PATH 上准备一个编码 Agent CLI(Claude Code / Codex / Gemini / Qwen)。规划中的 **Hover Cloud** 只负责**运行和监控**你本地编写的用例(并行跑、定时监控、flakiness 看板、失败时 AI 自愈),编写永远本地、免费;暂无上线时间。

## Reading

- Vibe coding vs. vibe testing: https://gethover.dev/blog/vibe-coding-vs-vibe-testing/
- AI-authored Playwright tests (why no AI in CI): https://gethover.dev/blog/ai-authored-playwright-tests/
- Bring your own CLI, no API key: https://gethover.dev/blog/bring-your-own-cli-no-api-key/
- What's new in the extension: https://gethover.dev/blog/whats-new-in-the-hover-vscode-extension/
